ety: for A. حمية, inf. n. of حَمِيَ 'to become very hot'
def: s.f. Indignation; scorn; — ardour, impetuosity, zeal; a nice sense of honour; care or concern for what is sacred, or what one is bound to honour or defend.
more: (from حما) s. f. Ardour, zeal, a nice sense of honour.

ety: act. part. n. of حمض 'to be sour,' &c.
def: adj. Acid, sour, sharp, pungent.
more: (part. act. of حمض Heb. ) adj. Sour.

ety: inf. n. of حمل 'to bear or carry,' &c.
def: s.m. A burden, load; — gestation, pregnancy; the fruit (of the womb), the young that is borne in the womb; — fruit (of a tree)
more: s. m. 1. A burden, a load. حمل کرنا ḥaml karnā, To load, to impute, to accuse, to ascribe. 2. The fruit (of the womb), pregnancy. 3. Fruit (of any kind). 4. ¶ ḥamal, Aries.
subs: v.t. To load; to ascribe or impute (to), to accuse
v.n. To be or become pregnant.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حمل (haml), from Arabic حَمْل (ḥaml).

ety: for A. حملة ḥamlat, fem.; v.n. fr. حمل
def: s.m. Charge, attack, assault, onset, storm; aggression, invasion; (fig.) concubitus; — a stroke, beat
more: n. m. 1. Assault; attack; onset; aggression; invasion; assault and battery. 2. Stroke; beat. Ek hamlah kūṭ-kar dūsrā hamlah kūṭ rahā hūṅ . One side is done, I am now doing the other. hamlah-āvar , n. m. An assailant. hamlah khafīf . A petty assault. hamlah k. , v. a. To assault; attack; offer violence. hamle kī dhamkī denā , v. a. To threaten an assault. hamle kī numāish . G. G. Show of assault.
s. m. 1. Attack, onset, assault, storm. 2. ( fig. ) Concubitus. حمله آور ḥamla-āwar, Attacking. حمله کرنا ḥamla karnā, To attack, to assault, to storm, to assail, attempt.
subs: adj. & s.m. Charging, attacking, &c.; — an assailant
(-par), To make an attack (on), to attack, assault, storm, assail; to attempt
ex: رہنما پر حملے کی مذمت کی گئی۔
rahnumā par hamle kī mazammat kī gaī.
The attack on the leader was condemned.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حَمْلَه (hamla).

def: s.m. Turning (a thing) anxiously in the mind; meditating, purposing; — anxious thought, anxiety, solicitude, grief, care; — purpose, design.
more: s. m. Grief, care, solicitude, turning a thing anxiously in the mind.
ex: ہَم سے نا کَبھی جُدا ہونا۔
ham se nā kabhī judā honā.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi हम (hama), हंम (haṃma), from Apabhramsa अम्हे (amhe), from Prakrit 𑀅𑀫𑁆𑀳𑁂 (amhe), from Sanskrit अस्मे (asme).

ety: v.n. fr. حمل 'to bear or carry'
def: s.m. A carrier of burdens, a porter; a pālkī-bearer.
more: adj. (superl. from حمل) used substantively, A porter, a carrier of burdens.

ety: for A. حمالة ḥammālat, fem. of ḥammāl
def: s.f. A female carrier of burdens.
more: s. f. A female porter or carrier of burdens.

def: s.m. A hot bath; a Turkish bath; a bagnio
more: n. m. حمم became hot. A Turkish bath; a bagnio. Ek hammām meṅ sab naṅge . Prov. One bath, all naked. (We are all in the same boat. Or, We all do the same thing). hammām k. , v. a. To bathe. hammām kī luṅgī , adj. lit . bath sheet. Free to all; common.
(from حمّ Heb. To become warm) s. m. A hot bath, bagnio. ¶ ḥamām, s. A pigeon.
subs: To take a hot bath, to bathe
s.f. 'A bath-sheet'; (hence, a thing) common to all, free.
wety: From Arabic حَمَّام (ḥammām).

def: s.m. The owner or keeper of a ḥammām or bath; an attendant at a bath.
more: n. m. The attendants at a bath.
s. m. A keeper of a bath or an attendant at a bath.

def: s.m. An ass-driver.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حِمَار (himār), from Arabic حِمَار (ḥimār).

def: n. m. Men of the same profession or calling.
adj. Red, of a red colour.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic حَمْرَاء (ḥamrāʔ), feminine singular of أَحْمَر (ʔaḥmar).

def: adj. & s.m. contrac. of ham-rāh, q.v.s.v. ham.
more: (هم and راه or ره Road) s. m. 1. A fellow-traveller. (Pers. plur. همرهان). 2. adv. With, together with, along with.

def: = P همراهي ham-rāhī, See s.v. هم ham.
more: (هم and راه or ره Road) s. m. 1. A fellow-traveller. (Pers. plur. همرهان). 2. adv. With, together with, along with.

def: = P همراه ham-rāh, See s.v. هم ham.
more: 1. s. f. Fellow-travelling, companionship, company, society in travelling. 2. s. m. f. Fellow-traveller, companion, comrade.

ety: prob. ham+tā = tāb or tāw, 'power, dignity,' &c.
def: adj. Equal; like, resembling; — s.m. An equal, a peer; a fellow
subs: adj. Without equal or like, peerless, incomparable.

def: s. f. Likeness, equality.
s.f. Likeness, resemblance; equality, parity.

ety: hām˚ = Zend hãma or hamā = S. सम; ˚ūn or un = wan S. वन्त्
def: s.m. Level ground; a plain; — a desert
more: s. m. A desert, a plain, a level ground.
subs: s.m. Desert-traveller; one who traverses (or has traversed) a desert or deserts.

def: adj. Plain, even, level, smooth; — proportional, symmetrical, well-made; — trained, disciplined; — suitable, worthy, fit
more: adj. Even, level, smooth. هموار کرنا hamwār karnā, To level, smooth; break in, train.
subs: v.t. To level, smooth, make plain; — to break in, to train
adj. Uneven; — unbroken, untrained, undisciplined; — unworthy; indecent; absurd; discordant; — disproportioned; unsuitable.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian هَمْوار.

def: adv. Always, continually, ever.
s.f. Evenness, levelness, flatness, smoothness, &c. (see hamwār).
more: s. f. Evenness, levelness, flatness, smoothness.

ety: for A. همزة hamzat, v.n. fr. همز 'to prick,' &c.
def: s.m. The orthographical sign hamza (ء), or the letter أ alif; — (in accounts) the sign for one-eighth of a ser.

n. m. 1. An orthographical mark (ء). 2. One-eighth of a seer. Hamza bhar ghī, nuqtā bhar āṭā . ( Fac .) A hamza weight of ghī and half a seer of flour. (In accounts the hamza stands for 1/8 of a seer and the dot for half a seer).

n. f. S. हानि See نقصان 1. Loss. 2. Injury; mischief. 3. Deficiency; blank. 4. Slaughter; murder. hān-pūran . An indemnity. hān pūran k. , v. a. To indemnify; reimburse. hān yā ṭoṭā uṭhānā , v. n. To suffer loss.

ety: Prk. थाणे; S. स्थाने, loc. of sthāna
def: adv. To or at the place or house (of; e.g. mere hāṅ, 'to or at my place or house,' Fr. chez moi).
intj. Yes; truly; — make haste! know! have a care! let it not be! far from it! keep off! make way!
s.m. Abandoning, abandonment, relinquishing; — escaping; — prowess, valour.

adv. S. आं Yes; aye. 1. Hāṅ karo yā nāṅh koro, yeh to karnā hī hogā? Say yes or say no, you will have to do it. 2. Phūl na pān, Debī lāṅ hāṅ . Prov. Nor flowers nor leaves on altar lays: 'Yes Goddess, Yes,' is all he says. hāṅ meṅ hāṅ milānā, hāṅ jī hāṅ jī k. , v. n. To say ditto; chime in with. 1. Sab ke sab hāṅ meṅ hāṅ milā rahe haiṅ . All men below seek their own end, with 'yes and ditto' voices blend. 2. Ūṅṭ bilaīyā le gaī to 'hāṅ jī hāṅ jī' kīje . Prov. If one should say the cat took the camel away, 'yes, yes,' you should chime in and say. 3. Tum to hāṅ jī ke naukar ho, 'Hāṅ jī' 'hāṅ jī' kīye jāte ho! 'Yes, Sir,' 'yes, Sir' is then your master, 'Yes, Sir,' 'yes, Sir' you only pratter. hāṅ nāṅ k. , v. a. To say yes and no; say no. Subah hī subah hāṅ nāṅ kartā hai. hāṅ hāṅ k . 1. To say yes. 2. To hum and haw. hāṅ hūṅ, hūṅ hāṅ ; Sant. heṅ hūṅ , n. f. Hum and haw. hāṅ hūṅ k. , To hum and haw.

ety: i.q. hūṅ, 'I am,' dialec. hauṅ, Panj. hāṅ
def: intj. Yes, aye; indeed, verily; by the by, forsooth
more: s. f. Loss, deficiency, harm, injury, slaughter.
interj. Make haste! know! have a care! let it not be! far from it.
1. adv. Yes, ay, indeed, by the by, forsooth. هان کہنا hāṅ kahna, To agree. 2. (contract. of يہان yahāṅ, q. v.) Here, hither, &c.
subs: Yes, Sir; oh, assuredly!; — s.f. Servile or obsequious assent (to everything), obsequiousness
or hāṅ-jī hāṅ-jī karnā, To say 'yes' (to everything), to approve and assent to with obsequiousness or adulation
To say 'yes,' to assent, agree, to yield
adv. Yes, yes; yes to be sure, &c.
or hāṅ-hūṅ (or hūṅ-hāṅ) karnā, To say 'yes, yes'; — to hum and haw
adv. (emphatic) Yes, yes indeed, yea verily.

def: adv. contrac. of yahāṅ, q.v.
perf. part. of next, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Killing, slaying; — killer, slayer, slaughterer, murderer (used as last member of compounds).
s.m. Striking; wounding; hurting, injuring; destroying; killing, slaying; — slaughter; destruction; — (in Arith.) multiplication
subs: adj. Of a cruel or murderons disposition cruel; murderous
(-ko), To wound; to kill, slay; to destroy.

def: (colloq.), s.m. per met. for nahān = snān, qq.v. s.m. Bathing, ablution; purification, lustration
s.f. = halas = haris, qq.v. s.f. The beam of a plough (i.q. haris).
part. Stricken; injured; slain.

ety: Prk. भंडो, or भंडओ; S. भाण्डः, or भाण्डकः
def: s.m. A (large) cooking-pot (whether of earth or metal), a cauldron.

n. m. S. हण्डा 1. A large earthen pot (بهاندَا). 2. A grass found on the banks of tanks and jhīls . It produces a little red flower. hanḍā k. , 1. To offer many earthen pots full of sweetmeats to a god. 2. To give a great feast after one's return from a pilgrimage.
s.m. A large earthen pot or boiler, a cauldron; — a species of grass (found on the banks of tanks and jhīls, and producing a little red flower)
more: s. m. A caldron, an earthen pot or boiler. هنڐا پهوڙنا hanḍā phoṛnā, v. a. ( lit. to break the pot) To disclose a secret, to let the cat out of the bag.
subs: 'To break the pot' (of); to disclose a secret, to let the cat out of the bag
(-kā), To offer earthen pots (full of sweetmeats, &c., to a god); to give a great feast (esp. on returning from a pilgrimage).

ety: Prk. भंडो, or भंडओ; S. भाण्डः, or भाण्डकः
def: s.m. A (large) cooking-pot (whether of earth or metal), a cauldron.
v. a. See ﮩـاندَنا

v.t. To cause to roam or to wander; to send or drive forth or out; to expel, banish; to disgrace by public exposure, &c. (as by taking one round a city on an ass); — to take or move round (syn. ghumānā).
more: v. a. 1. To banish, to expel, to drive out of a city, to disgrace (by publick punishment). 2. To move round.

ety: for A. هندسة handasat, the P. andāza arabicized
def: s.m. Geometry; — figure, or figures (of arithmetic); — arithmetic
more: (dakh. hindsa, from Pers. اندازه) s. m. Geometry, figures, arithmetick. هندسه دان handasa-dān, Geometrician, arithmetician, accountant.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian هَنْدَسَه (handasa), from Arabic هَنْدَسَة (handasa), itself a Middle Persian borrowing. Doublet of اَنْدَازَہ (andāza).

ety: Prk. भंडिआ; S. भाण्डिका, whence हण्डिका
def: s.f. A small earthen boiler or pot (i.q. hāṅḍī); — a relish
more: s. f. A small earthen pot (for boiling meat, &c.).
subs: 'The pot to boil'; (meat, &c.) to be cooked; (a scheme, &c.) to be concocted
v.n. The pot to be put on (the fire); to earn; — to take bribes; — to fill one's pockets
'The pot to be hot or boiling'; to earn; to be making money fast; to take bribes
s.f. Pots and pans, cooking utensils.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit हुण्डिका (huṇḍikā). Cognate with Punjabi ਹੁੰਡੀ (huṇḍī) / ہُنْڈِی (huṇḍī), Sindhi 𑋞𑋣𑋟𑋈𑋢 (huṇḍī) / हुंडी (huṇḍī) / هُنڊِي (huṇḍī), Assamese হুণ্ডী (hundi), Bengali হুন্ডি (hunḍi), Odia ହୁଣ୍ଡି (huṇḍi), Gujarati હૂંડી (hū̃ḍī), Marathi हुंडी (huṇḍī).

def: s.f. An earthen pot (for cooking, &c), a small cauldron;-globe (of a lamp)
more: s. f. A pot, a small caldron.
subs: A pot to boil over; — (fig.) to swell out
The pot to boil; — to talk, to gossip; — a plot or plan to be concocted or matured
To break an earthen pot (over the name of anyone; this is done on the departure of an obnoxious individual)
(-par), The pot to be put (on the fire)
'The pot to become warm or to be busy' (esp. by fraudulent means); to pilfer, to pocket, &c
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi هانڐي (hanḍy) (c. 1621), from Sanskrit हण्ड (haṇḍa). Cognate with Malvi हाँड़ी (hā̃ṛī), Bengali হাঁড়ী (hā̃ṛī), Marathi हंडा (haṇḍā).

def: n. f. S. हण्डी 1. An earthen cooking pot. 1. Jo kuchh hāṅḍī meṅ hogā, so rakābī meṅ āvegā . Prov. What's in the pot will come on the plate. 2. Bharī hāṅḍī jhīkḍā baḍerī lāge dhūāṅ . Bhoj. Prov. A pot full of shards, and such a smoke! (Great boast, little roast). 3. Jākī hanḍlī vā-kī manḍlī . Rus. Prov. Who owns the pot has friends a lot. 2. The globe of a lamp. hāṅḍī ubalnā , v. n. 1. To boil over (a pot). 2. To swell out. hāṅḍī paknā , v. n. 1. To boil (the pot). 2. To talk; gossip. 3. To be concocted or matured (a plan, plot). hāṅdī phoṛnā, yā kisī ke nām par hāṅdī phoṛnā , Tir. lit . to break an earthen pot. This is done on the departure of an obnoxious individual whose exit is looked upon as auspicious. O jekhan jaitā tekhani hunkā noūṅ se pachās hāṅḍī phūṭat . Tir. hāṅḍī chaṛhnā , v. n. To be put on the fire (the pot). hāṅḍī garm h. , v. n. To bag; pocket. hāṅḍī-vāl , n. m. A partner. bāolī yā dīvānī hāṅḍī , n. f. Hodge podge.

ety: prob. a Prk. form of bhāṇḍikā
def: s.f. = haṇḍī, q.v. s.f. An earthen pot (for cooking, &c), a small cauldron;-globe (of a lamp)
subs: A pot to boil over; — (fig.) to swell out
The pot to boil; — to talk, to gossip; — a plot or plan to be concocted or matured
To break an earthen pot (over the name of anyone; this is done on the departure of an obnoxious individual)
(-par), The pot to be put (on the fire)
'The pot to become warm or to be busy' (esp. by fraudulent means); to pilfer, to pocket, &c

ety: Prk. भंडिआ; S. भाण्डिका, whence हण्डिका
def: s.f. A small earthen boiler or pot (i.q. hāṅḍī); — a relish
subs: 'The pot to boil'; (meat, &c.) to be cooked; (a scheme, &c.) to be concocted
v.n. The pot to be put on (the fire); to earn; — to take bribes; — to fill one's pockets
'The pot to be hot or boiling'; to earn; to be making money fast; to take bribes
s.f. Pots and pans, cooking utensils.

def: s.f. An earthen pot (for cooking, &c), a small cauldron;-globe (of a lamp)
subs: A pot to boil over; — (fig.) to swell out
The pot to boil; — to talk, to gossip; — a plot or plan to be concocted or matured
To break an earthen pot (over the name of anyone; this is done on the departure of an obnoxious individual)
(-par), The pot to be put (on the fire)
'The pot to become warm or to be busy' (esp. by fraudulent means); to pilfer, to pocket, &c

def: n. f. 1. A small earthen pot ( hāṅḍī ). Sab kuttā Kāsī jaīheṅ to hanḍiyā ke chāṭi? Prov. If all the dogs on pilgrimage to Kāshī go, the pots to lick none left will be to. (Who is to cook the dinner?). 2. A relish. haṅḍiyā paknā , v. n. 1. To be cooked (meat, etc.). 2. To be concocted (a scheme). haṅḍiyā chaṛhnā, yā garm h , v. n. 1. To be put on the fire (the pot). 2. To earn; take bribes; bag; fill one's pocket. haṅḍiyā ḍoī , n. f. Pots and pans; cooking utensils.

v. n. To wander ( āvārah phirnā ). Hāṅḍe se ḍāṅḍā bhalā . Rus. Prov. Forced labor is better than wandering. hāṅḍnī nār , n. f. A gadabout (woman). Maryo ghar ghar hāṅḍnī nār! Die gadabout from house to house! ṭhālī hāṅḍnā , v. n. To wander about idly.

ety: hāṅḍ˚ prob. Prk. भंड(इ) = S. भण्ड(तें), rt. भण्ड्; — or = S. हिण्ड(ते), rt. हिण्ड्
def: v.n. To wander, ramble, stray about; to gad about (syn. bhaṭaknā, bhramnā).
more: v. n. To wander, to ramble.

ety: prob. Prk. हंतो, pres. part.; see huṅto
def: A dialec. (Mārwārī) postpn. of the gen. (i.q. kā; ker or kerā; rā, &c.).
ex: ہِنْدوں میں بُو رَہے گِی جَب تَک اِیمان کِی، تَخْتِ لَنْدَن تَک چَلے گِی تیغِ ہِنْدوسْتان کِی
hindõ mẽ bū rahe gī jab tak īmān kī, taxt-i landan tak cale gī teġ-i hindostān kī
As long as Hindoos have even an ounce of honesty and dignity-So long shall the sword of Hindustan flash before the throne of London.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian هندو (hindū).

def: s.m. Understanding, intellect, wisdom; — majesty, authority; dignity, gravity; — purpose, design; — strength, power, ability.

n. m. 1. Muscular or bodily strength. 2. Force; violence. Lambar-dār ne hamāre sāth hāṅgā kīyā. hāṅgā chhūṭnd , To be unnerved; to fail (one's strength). hāṅge , adv. Right or wrong; per fas et nefas. Gāṅṭh girah māṅ nāhīṅ kauḍī, hāṅge mūrakh ho sai modī! Rus. Not a farthing he has in his pocket, the grocer the fool must browbeat.
s.m. (dialec.) Coercion, force, violeuce; strength (of body)
subs: v.n. Strength to forsake (one) or to fail; to lose strength; to be or become unnerved
(-ke sāth, or hamāre sāth), To employ force or coercion (towards or with)
(obl. of hāṅgā), adv. By main force; right or wrong, per fas et nefas.

ety: prob. Zend hañ ( = ham, q.v.)+gāma, rt. gam = S. गम्; but cf. Zend aiwi-gāma
def: s.m. Season, time, period; — an assembly.
s.m. A convention, an assembly, a meeting; a crowd; — noise, tumult, commotion, confusion, uproar; sedition, disturbance, disorder; an affray; assault
more: s. m. 1. An assembly, a crowd. 2. Tumult, uproar, riot, sedition, disturbance, disorder. 3. Assault. هنگامه آرا hangāma-ārā, adj. Exciting tumult. هنگامه کرنا hangāma karnā, To raise a disturbance, to riot. هنگامه گير hangāma-gīr, adj. Tumultuous, &c.
subs: adj. Exciting tumult; creating a disturbance, &c.
s.f. The making a tumult or riot, &c.
v.n. To make a noise; to raise a disturbance, to make a riot
adj. & s.m. Tumultuous, uproarious, &c.; — a rope-dancer, tumbler.

def: n. m. 1. Tumult; uproar. 2. An affray; a disturbance. haṅgāma-pardāz , adj. Tumultuous; riotous. haṅgāma-pardāzī , Making a tumult or riot. haṅgāma k. , v. a. To raise a disturbance; make a riot.
adj. Temporal, ephemeral.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian هنگامی (hangāmī).

def: s.m. A shark; an alligator.
n. f. A sieve.

ety: or hāṅkī; prob. hāṅk(nā)+ī = Prk. एअव्वअइआ = S. (इ)तव्य+ता+इका
def: s.f. A sieve; — a vessel for making vermicelli with.
s.m. = hāhā, q.v.; — adv. redupl. of hāṅ, q.v. s.m. Laughing; ha! ha!
adv. Yes.
adj. = hāni-kar, q.v.

s.f. Relinquishment, abandonment, neglect, desertion; — loss, want, privation, absence (of); — decrease, deficiency, diminution; detriment, damage, harm, injury, mischief, wrong; destruction, slaughter, murder
subs: s.m. Compensation for loss, or harm, &c.; an indemnity
or hāni-kārak, or hāni-kārī, or hāni-kar, adj. Causing loss or injury, &c.; injurious, harmful, baneful, noxious, prejudicial, pernicious, mischievous; — s.m. An injurer; marrer, marplot
(-kī), To work the harm (of), to be harmful (to), to harm, injure, &c.
past conj. part. of hāni karnā, q.v.; — also corr. of hāni-kar, q.v.

def: adj. Wholesome; light, easy of digestion; pleasant, agreeable; inestimable; — possessed or acquired without trouble, easily obtained.
(dialec.), conj. part. of هننا hannā, q.v.

ety: act. part. n. of حنث 'to violate an oath'
def: s.m. A violator or non-performer of his oath; a perjurer.
more: (part. act. of حنث) s. m. A perjurer.

ety: han = Zend hañ = ham = S. सम्+jār = S. चर्
def: s.m. A straight road; a by-path; a short cut; — a true method; — way, rule, law; — habit, custom; conduct; — a mason's rule; a plumb-line, a level; any string or instrument used by builders in laying stones straight.
more: s. m. 1. A straight road, a true method. ناهنجار nā-hanjār, adj. Stray, wicked, unfortunate, rude, unpolite. 2. A masonʼs rule, a plumb-line, a level, &c.

ety: ḥanjara, for A. حنجرة, ḥanjarat, fem.; fr. حجر 'to prevent,' &c.
def: s.m. The windpipe, gullet, throat.

n. f. S. हङ्कार Call; cry ( pukār , 1). 1. Gūṅge ne ke māre hāṅk, āṅdhe ne ke dīje jhāṅk? Rus. Prov. Who heard the dumb man call, who saw the blind man peep? (The fool will never learn). 2. Ān gāoṅ āgi lāge, ān gāoṅ dhuāṅ, Koirī ke ḍār jāre, hāṅk pāre kuān . Riddle. Ans. A Huqqa. A village is on fire, another village smokes away, The Koeri's branch aburning is, the well begins to bray. hāṅk pukār , n. f. A hue and cry; hullabaloo. hāṅk pukār-ke kahnā , To say publicly so as to be heard by all. hāṅk mārnā , v. a. To call or bawl after ( pukārnā 3). hāṅkā hāṅk , n. m. Driving (an animal).

s.f. Calling aloud (to, -ko), bawling; call, cry, shout, halloo; — driving, urging onward; — driving off or away (by shouting or bawling); — renown, notoriety, being noised abroad, public talk (about); — rule, government
more: s. f. 1. Cry, call, bawling, calling to (loud). 2. Driving. هانک پکار hānk-pukār, s. f. Uproar, outcry. هانک مارنا hānk mārnā, To bawl after, to call to.
subs: s.m., or hāṅk-ā-hāṅkī, s.f. General and vehement (or confused) calling or bawling (by or to many); general outcry; — driving; — mutual pushing and driving
s.f. Cry, shout, hue and cry, outcry, uproar, general hubbub
To raise a cry or outcry, &c.; to protest
(hāṅk & pukār-ke, conj. part.), v.t. To say aloud or publicly, to shout out (or aloud) and say
v.n. To cry out, to shout; to call (to, -ko), to bawl (after).
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit हक्क (hakka, “calling to an elephant”). Cognate with Gujarati હાંક (hā̃k), Marathi हाक (hāk).

def: v. a. 1. To goad. 2. To drive away or out. 3. To drive or go on with. 4. To dilate or dwell on. Maiṅ kis se rahī? ūhī! roko zabāṅ ko! Chalo, chup raho! jhūṭī sachchī na hāṅko! Wom. Jān Sāhab. Now curb your tongue! why, goodness me! to whom did I e'er go? Come shut up there! and dont invent and go on lying so! 5. To ask an exorbitant price. Ajī! yeh to tum iske līye bahut dām hāṅkte ho . 6. To fan or use a fan. 7. To boast; brag; to pull a long bow. Khāṭ par paṛe dūnkī hāṅk rahe haiṅ . Stretched on his bed all day he brags and boasts away.

n. m. S. हंस 1. A goose; a swan. 1. Kulāh hans kī kavvā khaṛā utāre hai, Uchhal-ke meṅḍkī hāthī ke lāt māre hai . The times out of joint. Nazīr. The swan the crow insults by pulling off his crest, The elephant's kicked by the frog upon the chest. 2. The soul. Hansā chalale bhāg, keu nā saṅgai lāg . Bhoj. Prov. The soul hence speeds away, no one go with it may.
s.m. A duck; a goose; a swan; — the neck or throat; — the collar-bone; — (rustic) a collar (of gold or silver) worn round the neck (i.q. haṅslī, q.v.).
more: s. m. A duck, a goose, a swan.
s. (dakh.) A collar of gold or silver: see هنسلي haṅslī, sign. 2. and طوق t̤auḳ, sign. 2.

def: s.m. (f. -ī), A goose, gander; swan; duck; — flamingo; — the living soul; — one of the vital airs; — an epithet of Brahmā; of Vishṇu; of Śiva; of Kām-dev; of the Sun; of an unambitious and liberal monarch; of an ascetic or devotee of a particular order; — a kind of horse
more: s. m. 1. A duck, a goose. 2. ( met. ) Life. هنسگمني hans-gamanī or هنسگوني hans-gawanī, adj. Walking gracefully as the swan (a woman).
subs: s.m. Name of a herb which springs up on brick walls during the rains (it is used medicinally); the plant Maiden-hair, Adiantum capillus veneris (commonly found by the side of streams; Pers. syn. parsiyāvashān; vulg. pareshāwashān; parseʼoshāṅ); — name of a kind of rice
adj. 'Swan-like'; graceful as the swan (applied to the gait of a person)
s.f. A graceful woman (lit. 'walking like the swan')
s.m. The moving gracefully like a swan; gait of a swan or goose; graceful gait

def: s.m. (f. haṅsanuʼī), A laughing-stock.
adj. Provoking laughter; laughable, ridiculous.

1. A laugh; laughter. 1. Ek chijarvā gāṅveṅ bikāë, Hindū, Musalmāṅ donoṅ khāë, Ādhā gadhā, ādhā khassī, Ye suni-ke mohe aile haṅssī . Bhoj. Riddle. Ans. Kharbūzā . Marshmelon. A thing sold in the village street, Hindu and Moslem both it eat, A donkey and a goat each half. When I heard this it made me laugh. 2. Khāṅsī bairī choran kī, hāṅsī baiyar kī . Prov. A thief's foe is a cough, a woman's foe a laugh. 3. Kyāhaṅsī ātī hai mujh ko hazrat-i-insān par, Fel-i-bad to khud kareṅ, lānat kareṅ shaitān par! Inshā. Lord of creation, man to laughter moves me just, Himself he evil does, the devil he curse must. 2. Jesting; flirtation. haṅsī uṛānā , v. a. To make fun or a jest of; to ridicule. haṅsī samajhnā, haṅsī meṅ le jānā , v. n. To take as a joke. Tum to haṅsī meṅ le jāte ho . Double entendre. haṅsī k. , v. a. 1. To laugh and jest. 2. To flirt; jest with. haṅsī khushī , adv. Gladly. haṅsī meṅ , adv. In jest. haṅsī meṅ uṛānā , v. a. To turn off with a joke. haṅsī meṅ khaṅsī h. , v. n. lit . to go from laughing to coughing. To go from words to blows.
s.f. (rustic or dialec.), Laughing, laughter, &c. (i.q. haṅsī, q.v.).
more: s. f. Laughter, laughing, joke, derision.

v. n. S. हसन; Pr. hassaī he laughs. 1. ( haṅsī ānā ) To laugh. Waqt kā ronā be-vaqt ke haṅsne se achchhā . Prov. Better weep in season than laugh out of season. 2. To laugh at; deride. 1. Jo haṅsegā voh haṅsāyā jāëgā . Prov. Who laughs will be laughed at. 2. Goiṭhā jale, gobar haṅse . Prov. Dried cow pats burn, the fresh dung laughs. 3. Sir turrā muqqaish kā, aur gorā kāman gāt, Khaṛī khaṛī rove haṅse, kanth na pūchhe bāt . Riddle. Ans. Shamā . A candle. A golden crest upon her head, a body fair she's got, She ever stands and weeps and laughs, her husband heeds her not. 3. To laugh and be merry. Ro-ke pūchh le, haṅs-ke uṛā de . Prov. With tears shed learnt your mind, alaughing him you find. 4. To jest or flirt with. 1. Haṅste hī ghar baste haiṅ . Prov. 'Tis laughter fills a house. (From flirting comes marrying.). (Flirt in jest, marry in earnest) 2. Haṅsiye dūr, paṛausī ke nāh . Flirt with strangers, not with your neighbours. 5. To be in jest, not earnest. Maiṅ to haṅstā thā . haṅsoṛ', haṅsokaṛ . One who is always laughing. Pat nāhīṅ nār haṅsokaṛ kī . Rus. song. A woman. who is always laughing is held in no respect.
v.n. (rustic or dialec.) = هنسنا haṅsnā, q.v.

n. m. The sound made by a strong and sudden compression of the lips. Hap hap, jhap jhap khāte hān, dhandā karte taje pirān . Rus. Prov. Gobble gobble, gulping greedily, Work him and his breath goes speedily. hap k., hap kar jānā , v. a. 1. To gulp down. 2. To eat up.

n. m. 1. A name of Mahādeva. 1. Pattharpūje Harmileṅ to maiṅ pūjūṅ pahāṛ, Us patthar se chakkī bhalī jo pīs khilāë sansār . Kabīr. Har jaise ko taisā . Prov. God gives to each as he does. 2. Water. 3. A frog. 4. A snake. Har bolā, har ne sunā, har gaë har ke pās, Woh har to har meṅ gaë, woh har bhaë nirās . The croaking frog the snake doth hear, and t'wards the frog he goes, The frog into the water jumps, despair the snake now shows. har gangā k . To take Har's name while bathing. har-bhajan , n. m. Tū to harjāī hai, apnā bhī yehī taur sahī, Tū nahīṅ, aur sahī; aur nahīṅ, aur sahī . Amānat. You wander, wanton, every where, I too will do as well, If not you then a new one, or a third will s…

Whenever. هرايک har ek, Every one, each. هرباب har bāb, Every chapter, all sciences, every circumstance. هربابي har-bāb-ī, Master of knowledge of every kind; a factotum. هربار har bār, Every time. هرجاٴي har-jā-ī, adj. ety: Ap. Prk. अडं and अडअं (with euphonic h inserted); S. क+रं or र+कं
def: suff. (f. -ī), added to infinitives to form, 1˚ part. fut. act., or 2˚ nouns of agency (the inf. aff., in Western Hindī, generally takes the form an or ne = Prk.अणअ or अणिअ = S. अनीय; in East. H. ani or ni = Prk. अणिअ, &c.), e.g. karan-hār, or karne-hār or hārā (E. H. karani-hār or hārā), 'about to do; one who is going to do; doer'; — hon-hār, or honi-hār, 'what is to be; possible; future.' In W. H. this suff. frequently takes the form wālā or wārā (euphonic w taking the place of h), e.g. dene-wālā or dene-wārā (f. -wālī, &c.), 'about to give; one who is about to give; a giver.' The suff. hār and hārā are frequently confounded with the secondary suff. wālā, q.v.; e.g. lakaṛ-hārā, 'a woodman.' (It would perhaps be more correct to regard these suff. as being ˚anhār, ˚anehār, ˚anihār, &c., and to consider them as attached to the roots of verbs; e.g. mar-anhār, fr. rt. mar; but the popular notion is that the suffixes are hār, &c., and are joined to infinitives; and hence, probably, the confusion between these suffixes and the suffix wālā.)
wety: Inherited from Prakrit हारि (hāri), from Sanskrit हारि (hāri, “defeat; losing a game”).

def: s.f. (dialec.) = harrā, or haṛ qq.v. s.m. Attack, assault, onset, storm
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian هر (har). Doublet of سب (sab).

def: s.m. Taking, seizing, &c.; — a porter, carrier; — war, battle; — a necklace, a string (of pearls, &c.); a garland or chaplet (of flowers); a wreath; — a flock (of cattle); — (in Arith.) divisor; denominator (of a fraction)
more: (in compos.) means the performer of any act, and corresponds frequently to the English terminations er or ing; as, بہارآٴي نه ديکهن هارآيا انارآيا نه چاکهن هارآيا Bahār āʼī na dekhan-hār āyā; anār āyā, na chākhan-hār āyā. The spring is arrived, but no one is come to behold it; the pomegranates are ready, but there is no one to taste them.

1. s. m. (s. हार ) A necklace of pearls, &c. a wreath, a chaplet (of flowers, worn as a necklace). 2. (for हारि ) A flock (of cattle). 3. Pasturage. 4. s. f. Loss, forfeiture, discomfiture. 5. part. act. (from هارنا) Being overcome. هارماننا يا مان لينا hār-mānnā, or -mān lenā, To give up a dispute, to give up in despair, to acknowledge all lost. هارجيت hār jīt, Gambling, hazard. هار جيت کرنا hār jīt karnā, To gamble.
subs: To put on a garland, or chaplet, or wreath, &c.
(kisī-ke gale-meṅ), To throw a garland (round the neck of anyone); — to give (a maiden) in marriage
(-ke), To be or become as a necklace (to), to hang round the neck (of a person).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit हारि (hāri), from Sanskrit हारि (hāri, “defeat; losing a game”).

def: n. m. A mud oven on which milk is boiled.

adj. S. हरित. 1. Green; verdant; fresh. 1. Gadhe ko harā hī harā sūjhe . Prov. In the donkey's eyes all seems green. 2. Kaho jo Rām kā kavan sā rūp hai, Yuvā dekhā yā birdh, bālā? Kaho jo Rām kavan isthūl hai, Harā, pīlā, ki lāl, kālā? Kabīr. Tell me, can you what is Ram's body like, Or young, or old, or infantine belike? What aspect, what complexion doth he wear? Or green, or yellow, red, or black, or fair? 2. Blooming; flourishing. 3. Green (a wound). 4. Half raw or unbaked. har'ā , n. m. 1. Green color. 2. Green food for cattle. harā bāg , n. m. 1. A green garden. 2. ( Met .) Delusive hope. 3. ( Bot .) Herbaceous. harā bharā, harī bharī , adj. 1. Luxuriant; fruitful. 2. Prosperous; happy. Terī god sadā harī bharī rahe! Benediction. harā h., haryānā , v. n. 1. To be green. 2. To be promising. Ḍūbe rupaë hare ho gaë . 3. To be refreshed, exhilarated. Nahāne se jī harā ho jātā hai . 4. To be overjoyed, pleased, glad. Māṅ beṭe ko dekh-kar harī ho gaī . 5. To break out afresh. hare bhare raho! May you live and prosper! Heaven bless you! harī tarkārī , n. f. Vegetables; green meat. harī fasl . Green crops, as cucumbers, potatoes, carrots, and other garden produce. hare meṅ ānkheṅ h., yā phūlnā , v. n. To be prodigal, extravagant.
more: n. m. Dispersion (of an army, assembly, etc.); general jail delivery.

adj. (f. harī-bharī), Luxuriant; fruitful; — overgrown; — prosperous, happy

H. ani or ni = Prk. अणिअ, &c.), e.g. karan-hār, or karne-hār or hārā (E. H. karani-hār or hārā), 'about to do; one who is going to do; doer'; — hon-hār, or honi-hār, 'what is to be; possible; future.' In W. H. this suff. frequently takes the form wālā or wārā (euphonic w taking the place of h), e.g. dene-wālā or dene-wārā (f. -wālī, &c.), 'about to give; one who is about to give; a giver.' The suff. hār and hārā are frequently confounded with the secondary suff. wālā, q.v.; e.g. lakaṛ-hārā, 'a woodman.' (It would perhaps be more correct to regard these suff. as being ˚anhār, ˚anehār, ˚anihār, &c., and to consider them as attached to the roots of verbs; e.g. mar-anhār, fr. rt. mar; but the popular notion is that the suffixes are hār, &c., and are joined to infinitives; and hence, probably, the confusion between these suffixes and the suffix wālā.)
more: (in compos.) denotes the performer of any act, dealer in any article. It is often represented in English by the word man, or the termination er; as لکڙهارا lakaṛ-hārā, A woodman. مارني هارا mārne-hārā, A striker.

more: adj. حرم was unlawful. 1. Illegitimate; adulterous. 2. Unlawful; forbidden; unclean; impure. 1. Khudā kā mārā harām, apnā mārā halāl . Prov. Struck dead by Heaven unlawful meat, slaughtered by man it is food meet. 2. Do mullāoṅ meṅ murgī harām . Prov. Between two reverend doctors the fowl becomes unlawful meat (through being torn to pieces in the struggle for possession). 3. Harām khānā, aur shalgam! Hin. Prov. Eat forbidden food, and only a turnip! (One may as well be hanged for a sheep as for a lamb). 3. Wicked; wrong; nefarious. harām haddīs , Lawful and unlawful. harām-khor, harām-khānā , n. m. Opp. of halāl-khor . 1. A venal or corrupt person; one who lives on the wages of iniquity; one addicted to taking bribes, or other unlawful gains. 2. An idle, slow, or lazy person who does not perform the task for which he is paid. harām-khorī , n. f. 1. Peculation; embezzlement; malversation;…

n. m. Adultery (Sant. lāṭ ); illicit intercourse. 1. Harām koṭhe chaṛh-kar pukārtā hai . Prov. The harlot cries from the housetop. 2. Harām chālīs ghar le-kar ḍūbtā hai . Prov. The adulterer sinks with forty ruined households on his back. 3. Harām meṅ baṛā mazā hai . Prov. Sin is sweet.

def: adv. In a flurry, flurriedly, confusedly; in agitation or consternation; in hot haste, hurriedly, &c.
v. n. 1. To hurry. 2. To start up from a troubled dream or sleep. 3. To rumble (the bowels).

ety: haṛbaṛ˚, perhaps fr. S. हूल (rt. हूल्) by redupl. or alliteration (cf. hālnā); — or akin to khalbal˚, or ghabr(ānā), qq.v.; — ˚ānā = ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. aug.)+nā
def: v.n. To be flurried, to be confused, to be agitated; to be in consternation; — to be in a hurry; to hurry; to be precipitate; — to start up from a troubled dream or sleep; to be fidgety, or restless; — to rumble (as the bowels); — v.t. To flurry, to confuse, to perplex.
more: v. n. To be confused, to hurry.

ety: haṛbaṛ, as in haṛbaṛānā, q.v.+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. Tumult, confusion, uproar, commotion, disorder, pother, hubbub, hurly-burly, riot, mutiny; alarm, flurry; hurry, haste, precipitation, hastiness, rashness
more: s. f. Hurry, alarm, uproar, riot, disorder, hastiness, hubbub.
subs: To raise a commotion, create a disturbance or hubbub, &c.
(-ko), To be flurried, to be in a state of alarm or agitation; to run or escape helter-skelter

def: n. f. 1. Hurry; haste ( jaldī , 2). 2. Disorder; confusion. haṛbaṛī paṛnā, yā lagnā , v. n. To escape or run helter-skelter.
adj. Hasty; hurried; fidgety. haṛbaṛiyā , n. m. One who is always in a hurry.
adj. & s.m. Easily flurried or agitated; hurried; hasty, precipitate; irritable; fidgety; — one who is easily flurried, &c.; one who is always in a hurry.

r: harbauṅg', harbaṅg, harbhoṅg, harboṅg kā rāj, haṛbam
def: n. m. lit . the rule of Harbang. Civil disorders; maladministration: anarchy. Chār jāt gāveṅ harbom, Ahīr, Ḍaphālī, Dhobī, Ḍom . Prov. Cowherds, washers, drummers, singers, With these classes noise e' er lingers. harbauṅg machānā , v. a. To make a noise, din, or row.
The chick-pea, 'gram,' Cicer arietinum (syn. ćanā).
v. n. (dakh.) To hurry.
The chick-pea, 'gram,' Cicer arietinum (syn. ćanā).
more: s. (dakh.) Bengal gram: see چنا chanā.

def: s.m. = هربونگ harboṅg, q.v. s.m. Disorder, anarchy, confusion; uproar, noise; — name of a certain very foolish Rāja, proverbial for his stupidity and misrule, who is said to have flourished at one time at Jhūnsī (called also Harbong-pur) near Allahabad
subs: s.m. 'Harbong's government,' civil disorder, maladministration, anarchy
To create disorder or confusion; to make an uproar, or din, &c.

def: adj. & s.m. Relating to war; warlike, hostile; — a warrior.
more: adj. Warlike, relating to war. s. A warriour.
